lessons.trigger: When dealing with questions requiring real-time or external information that isn't accessible during the session.
lessons.insight: When real-time or dynamically changing data (e.g., GitHub stars) cannot be accessed, redirect users to trusted sources like the system's official page. Avoid assumptions that can lead to inaccuracies.

lessons.trigger: Attempted to read a non-existent file (hello).
lessons.insight: When reading a file that does not exist, inform the user clearly and confirm their intention to create it or specify another path.

lessons.trigger: Building the Snake game project structure in the `my-react-app` folder.
lessons.insight: When initializing a new feature (like the Snake game), breaking down components into independent functional units (e.g., MainMenu and GameBoard) and keeping styles, logic, and components modular within directories promotes clarity and reusability.
